title: pinus ponderosa subsp. scopulorum
intro: Pinus ponderosa subsp. scopulorum, also known as Rocky Mountain ponderosa pine, is a subspecies of evergreen tree native to the western United States and southwestern Canada. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been shown to aid in wound healing and reduce pain and swelling. The health benefits of Pinus ponderosa subsp. scopulorum include its potential to reduce the risk of respiratory infections, improve cardiovascular health, and support immune function. The plant can be prepared for herbal use through infusions, decoctions, and essential oils, which can be used to promote relaxation, reduce stress, and soothe respiratory issues.
